mission.title: Install and Configure SurrealDB
mission.description: SurrealDB is a scalable, distributed, collaborative, document-graph database designed for the real-time web. It is ideal for applications requiring flexible data models and high performance. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and management compared to traditional databases.

steps.title: Add the SurrealDB Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official SurrealDB Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add surrealdb https://charts.surrealdb.com

helm repo update

```

> This is the official repository for SurrealDB Helm charts.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install SurrealDB with Helm
steps.description:
This command installs SurrealDB in the specified namespace with the desired version.

```bash

helm install surrealdb surrealdb/surrealdb --namespace surreal --create-namespace --version 3.0.2

```

> Installs SurrealDB in a new namespace called 'surreal'.

steps.title: Create a sample configuration
steps.description:
This step creates a sample configuration for SurrealDB to run properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: surrealdb.com/v1
kind: SurrealDB
metadata:
  name: surrealdb-sample
  namespace: surreal
spec:
  db: 'test'
EOF

```

> Creates a sample database configuration.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: surrealdb.com/v1
kind: SurrealDB
metadata:
  name: surrealdb-sample
  namespace: surreal
spec:
  db: 'test'
EOF

steps.title: Verify SurrealDB installation
steps.description:
This command checks if the SurrealDB pods are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n surreal

```

> You should see the SurrealDB pod in a Running state.

uninstall.title: Uninstall SurrealDB
uninstall.description:
This step removes the SurrealDB installation and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall surrealdb --namespace surreal

kubectl delete namespace surreal

```

> Uninstalls the SurrealDB release.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade SurrealDB
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your data. This step will upgrade SurrealDB to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl cp surrealdb/surrealdb-sample:/path/to/data /local/backup

helm upgrade surrealdb surrealdb/surrealdb --namespace surreal --version 3.0.2

kubectl get pods -n surreal

```

> Backup your data before proceeding.
> Upgrades SurrealDB to the specified version.
> Verify that the upgraded pod is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to database'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The SurrealDB pod may not be running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the pod status with 'kubectl get pods -n surreal' and review logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n surreal'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes cluster may not have enough resources allocated for SurrealDB.

**Fix:**
Increase the resource limits in the Helm chart values or scale your cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Database not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified database in the configuration may not exist.

**Fix:**
Ensure the database name in your configuration matches the one created in SurrealDB.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Permission denied'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** RBAC settings may not allow access to the SurrealDB resources.

**Fix:**
Check and update your RBAC settings to grant the necessary permissions.

versionNotes.changes: Improved performance for concurrent writes, added support for multi-tenancy.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old authentication method, which is no longer supported.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 2.x, ensure to migrate your authentication to the new method.

resolution.summary: You should see the SurrealDB pod running with the command 'kubectl get pods -n surreal' showing a status of 'Running'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: surrealdb.com/v1
kind: SurrealDB
metadata:
  name: surrealdb-sample
  namespace: surreal
spec:
  db: 'test'
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure your Kubernetes cluster meets the resource requirements and that you have the necessary tools installed.
